Document:
# Gas Mining
Gas mining is a crucial part of the station's livability, economy, and providing valuable resources that are used in various industries.
Note that gas mining is a step up from regular mining, and it is not recommended to start with it if you are new to mining in general. It is recommended to get familiar with regular mining first before trying out gas mining.
## Equipment & Preparation Before you can start mining, you need a a few things: - A [color=cyan]Gas Analyzer[/color]: This device reveals the amount of gas trapped in the asteroid. - At least one [color=cyan]Gas miner[/color]: This machine is the primary tool for extracting gas from asteroids. - Two [color=cyan]Multi-chanel Transfer Nodes[/color] (MCTNs): These are used to transfer gas from the mining site to the shuttle, and from the shuttle to the station you will be selling gas to. - [color=cyan]MV Cables[/color]: These are used to connect the gas miner(s) to the MCTNs.
## Mining To start mining, you need to: - Anchor the gas miner(s) onto the asteroid - Connect the gas miner(s) to the MCTN (on the asteroid) with MV cables and pipes. - Interact with the MCTNs and select the appropriate pipe line and MV line.
Note that the MCTNs need to be anchored and close to each others before you can connect them together.
- While waiting, you will notice that the mining process will start to slow down significantly as time goes on. This is because the gas is being depleted. It is usually inefficient to completely drain an asteroid. Everyone has their preferences, but 10kPa is a good point to stop.
### Storage To have a profitable mining trip, you need to make sure that you have enough storage for the gas you are mining. There are two main options for storing gas: canisters and gas chambers.
Gas chambers are a more efficient way to store gas, but they are also more expensive and require more preparation. Canisters are cheaper and easier to use, but they take more work to manage and transport.
